title: Tangy Fluffy Russian Black Beans Stew
description: A protein-packed, tangy Russian-inspired stew featuring fermented black beans cooked with a fragrant bhuna of spices and finished with a splash of vinegar for a fluffy texture.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, and ground coriander; cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste, mixing well to coat the spices.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Add the fermented black beans, reduce heat, and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
Stir in the apple cider vinegar, then season with salt and black pepper.
Continue to simmer for another 5 minutes, ensuring the stew reaches at least 165°F for safety.
Remove from heat and let the stew sit for 5 minutes; the liquid will thicken into a fluffy consistency.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh herbs or a dollop of low-fat yogurt.
